channel.title: AI at Meta Blog
channel.description: AI research, open source, and applications from Meta

item.title: Introducing Muse Spark 1.1
item.description: Introducing Muse Spark 1.1

item.title: Introducing Muse Image and Muse Video
item.description: Introducing Muse Image and Muse Video

item.title: From Brain Waves to Words: Brain2Qwerty Offers a New Path to Communication Without Surgery
item.description: From Brain Waves to Words: Brain2Qwerty Offers a New Path to Communication Without Surgery

item.title: Introducing Muse Spark: Scaling Towards Personal Superintelligence
item.description: Introducing Muse Spark: Scaling Towards Personal Superintelligence

item.title: Scaling How We Build and Test Our Most Advanced AI
item.description: Scaling How We Build and Test Our Most Advanced AI

item.title: How Alta Daily Uses Meta’s Segment Anything to Reimagine the Digital Closet
SAM 3.1: Faster and More Accessible Real-Time Video Detection and Tracking With Multiplexing and Global Reasoning
Introducing TRIBE v2: A Predictive Foundation Model Trained to Understand How the Human Brain Processes Complex Stimuli
Mapping the World's Forests with Greater Precision: Introducing Canopy Height Maps v2
item.description: Today, in partnership with the World Resources Institute, we’re announcing Canopy Height Maps v2 (CHMv2): an open source model and world-scale maps generated with it.

item.title: Reducing Government Costs and Increasing Access to Greenspaces in the United Kingdom with DINO
item.description: Meta's DINOv2 model is enhancing reforestation efforts around the world. Learn how the UK government is using DINO to help reduce costs and increase access to greenspaces.

item.title: The Universities Space Research Association Applies Segment Anything Model for Responding to Flood Emergencies
item.description: The Universities Space Research Association and Meta are collaborating to help support water observing systems set up by the U.S. Geological Survey.

item.title: How DINO and SAM are Helping Modernize Essential Medical Triage Practices
item.description: By leveraging advanced AI models, teams at the University of Pennsylvania are aiming to bring cutting-edge automation to emergency response.

item.title: Introducing SAM Audio: The First Unified Multimodal Model for Audio Separation
item.description: SAM Audio transforms audio processing by making it easy to isolate any sound from complex audio mixtures using natural, multimodal prompts — whether through text, visual cues, or marking time segments.

item.title: How Conservation X Labs Is Using Segment Anything Model 3 for Endangered Wildlife Monitoring
item.description: Start-ups like Conservation X Labs are using Meta’s Segment Anything Models to bolster on-the-ground conservation expertise.

item.title: ExecuTorch Adoption in Reality Labs: Powering On-Device AI Across Meta Devices
item.description: ExecuTorch, Meta’s open source, lightweight, and efficient inference engine, has been instrumental in enabling on-device AI capabilities across Meta’s family of apps.

item.title: Introducing SAM 3D: Powerful 3D Reconstruction for Physical World Images
item.description: This release introduces two new state-of-the-art models: SAM 3D Objects for object and scene reconstruction, and SAM 3D Body for human body and shape estimation.

item.title: Omnilingual ASR: Advancing Automatic Speech Recognition for 1,600+ Languages
item.description: We’re introducing Meta Omnilingual Automatic Speech Recognition, a suite of models providing automatic speech recognition capabilities for over 1,600 languages.

item.title: Agents Rule of Two: A Practical Approach to AI Agent Security
The Building Blocks of Agentic AI: From Kernels to Clusters
item.description: At PyTorch Conference 2025 in San Francisco, we unveiled five new projects spanning kernel languages, distributed systems, reinforcement learning, agentic frameworks, and edge AI deployment.

item.title: How Llama and Oracle are helping Instituto PROA kickstart careers for students in Brazil
item.description: Instituto PROA, a nonprofit organization in Brazil, has transformed its job preparation process for young candidates by leveraging Llama and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.

item.title: How DINOv3 is helping World Resources Institute restore forests and farms globally
item.description: WRI and the Bezos Earth Fund used DINOv3 to develop an algorithm to accurately count individual trees from drone and satellite imagery.

item.title: Small robots, mighty vision: NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory's DINOv2-enabled robot rovers and the future of planetary exploration
item.description: Using DINOv2, the team at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory built a convenient robot operating system interface for robotic tasks.

item.title: DINOv3: Self-supervised learning for vision at unprecedented scale
item.description: DINOv3 scales self-supervised learning for images to create universal vision backbones that achieve absolute state-of-the-art performance across diverse domains, including web and satellite imagery.

item.title: How Llama helps Biofy Technologies in the fight against antibiotic resistance
item.description: Brazil, the biotech company Biofy Technologies has developed a groundbreaking platform using Llama that reduces diagnostic time for antibiotic resistance from five days to less than four hours.

item.title: How Upwork helps freelancers win more work with Llama
item.description: Upwork, one of the world’s largest work marketplaces, is using Llama to power Uma, its mindful AI, to help freelancers land jobs faster and more confidently.

item.title: Joining forces with AWS on a new program to help startups build with Llama
item.description: We're joining forces with Amazon Web Services to announce a new program that will provide resources and support to 30 promising startups in the U.S. that are building with Llama.

item.title: Modeling natural conversational dynamics with Seamless Interaction
How Llama helps drive engineering efficiency at a major Australian bank
item.description: ANZ, one of Australia's Big Four banks, is driving engineering efficiency with Llama.

item.title: Announcing the inaugural Llama Startup Program cohort
item.description: At Meta, we believe in the potential of early-stage startups to drive innovation in the generative AI market, and through the Llama Startup Program, we aim to lower the barrier to entry for getting started with Llama models.

item.title: Introducing the V-JEPA 2 world model and new benchmarks for physical reasoning
item.description: We’re excited to share V-JEPA 2, the first world model trained on video that enables state-of-the-art understanding and prediction, as well as zero-shot planning and robot control in new environments.

item.title: Inside Aria Gen 2: Explore the cutting-edge tech behind the device
item.description: Today, we’re excited to share more about the technology inside Aria Gen 2. This includes an in-depth overview of the form factor, audio capabilities, battery life, upgraded cameras and sensors, on-device compute, and more.

item.title: Introducing the Llama Startup Program
item.description: We’re excited to announce the Llama Startup Program, a new initiative to empower early stage startups to innovate and build generative AI applications with Llama.
